feedback_summary: Feedback repeatedly pointed out missing evidence and asked the learner to underline key passage sentences before answering.
practice_summary: Practice tasks scored 0.5 and 1.0, and the post-practice state improved reading from 0.132 to 0.306.
posttest_change: Reading QA average improved from 0.46 to 0.625.
ppt_message: Adaptive guidance used explicit reading weakness to choose targeted evidence-extraction practice and produced a positive posttest gain.

feedback_summary: Generic mode used the same general instruction: read carefully, find key evidence, answer briefly.
practice_summary: Fixed practice still improved evidence-focused answers in the posttest.
posttest_change: Generic guidance improved learner_a reading from 0.46 to 0.75, higher than adaptive in this case.
ppt_message: This case prevents overclaiming: a simple generic checklist can be a strong baseline.

fairness_summary: The same learner and same task type started from the same pretest score across all modes.
practice_summary: No guidance skipped practice; generic and adaptive both practiced on reserve reading tasks.
ppt_message: This case illustrates the controlled comparison design: identical baseline, different intervention, fixed posttest measurement.
